task_completion.description: The agent opens the diagnostic log file to identify the corrupt assets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/bc_startup_errors.log'.

task_completion.description: The agent creates the quarantine directory on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A folder named 'Quarantined_Assets' is visible on the Desktop or in a file manager navigating to '/home/ga/Desktop/'.

task_completion.description: The agent moves the loose files for the BadShip_v1 asset to the quarantine directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Files named 'BadShip_v1.x', 'BadShip_v1.ini', and 'BadShip_v1_diffuse.png' are visible inside the 'Quarantined_Assets' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent moves the RustyBuoy folder to the quarantine directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A folder named 'RustyBuoy' is visible inside the 'Quarantined_Assets' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent moves the loose files for the CorruptTankerC asset to the quarantine directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Files named 'CorruptTankerC.x' and 'CorruptTankerC.ini' are visible inside the 'Quarantined_Assets' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent does not move valid assets to the quarantine directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Quarantined_Assets' directory does NOT contain 'ValidShip' or 'GoodBuoy'.

task_completion.description: The agent creates a report listing the removed assets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'report.txt' is visible in the 'Quarantined_Assets' directory, and if opened, it lists 'BadShip_v1', 'RustyBuoy', and 'CorruptTankerC'.

integrity.description: The agent actually moved the files from the original directory rather than creating fake empty files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands show 'mv' being used, or a file manager shows files being cut/pasted from '/opt/bridgecommand/Models/'. The files in the quarantine directory should have appropriate icons/sizes for 3D models and images, not empty text files.

integrity.description: The agent identified the corrupt assets by reading the log file, not by guessing or using prior knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent spends time viewing the 'bc_startup_errors.log' file before moving any assets.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the report file based on the identified assets.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing the asset names into a text editor or using 'echo' commands to create 'report.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The three corrupt assets that must be quarantined are: 1) 'BadShip_v1' (loose files: BadShip_v1.x, BadShip_v1.ini, BadShip_v1_diffuse.png), 2) 'RustyBuoy' (a folder), and 3) 'CorruptTankerC' (loose files: CorruptTankerC.x, CorruptTankerC.ini). Valid assets 'ValidShip' and 'GoodBuoy' must NOT be quarantined. The quarantine directory is '/home/ga/Desktop/Quarantined_Assets'. The report file must be at '/home/ga/Desktop/Quarantined_Assets/report.txt' and list the three corrupt assets.
